seo.title: 100 VPS Hosting (Hetzner, etc.) resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical VPS Hosting (Hetzner, etc.) resources for engineers.

content.intro: This resource guide provides a technical roadmap for developers migrating from managed PaaS environments to self-managed VPS infrastructure like Hetzner, OVH, or Contabo. It focuses on production-grade security, automated deployment workflows, and cost-optimized scaling strategies.

items.title: Hetzner Cloud (hcloud) CLI
items.description: Install the hcloud-cli to manage server lifecycles, floating IPs, and private networks directly from your terminal or CI/CD pipelines.

items.title: Terraform Hetzner Cloud Provider
items.description: Use the official Hetzner Cloud provider for Terraform to define your infrastructure as code, ensuring reproducible server environments and network topologies.

items.title: Cloud-init for Automated Setup
items.description: Write YAML-based cloud-config files to automate user creation, package installation, and SSH key injection during the first boot of a VPS.

items.title: Hetzner Storage Boxes
items.description: Mount external storage via Samba, NFS, or WebDAV for large media files or database backups, keeping your primary VPS disk lean.

items.title: Contabo Object Storage
items.description: An S3-compatible storage solution for European-based data residency, ideal for storing application assets and cold backups at low cost.

items.title: Ansible Server Hardening Playbooks
items.description: Use community-vetted playbooks like 'dev-sec.ssh-hardening' to apply industry-standard security configurations across multiple VPS instances.

items.title: Scaleway VPC and Private Networks
items.description: Configure private network interfaces to allow internal communication between app servers and databases without exposing traffic to the public internet.

items.title: Packer for Golden Images
items.description: Build pre-configured Ubuntu images with your stack pre-installed to reduce the spin-up time for new nodes in a cluster.

items.title: OVHcloud Load Balancer
items.description: Implement a managed load balancer to distribute traffic across several VPS instances in different data centers for high availability.

items.title: Hetzner Robot API
items.description: For developers using bare metal (Dedicated Servers), use the Robot API to manage IP assignments, resets, and rescue system boots.

items.title: Caddy Server for Automatic SSL
items.description: Deploy Caddy as a reverse proxy to handle Let's Encrypt certificates automatically with zero-config HTTPS for all your subdomains.

items.title: Tailscale for Private Management
items.description: Restrict SSH and database ports to a Tailscale mesh network, effectively removing your management ports from the public internet.

items.title: UFW (Uncomplicated Firewall) Configuration
items.description: Implement a strict 'deny all' incoming policy, specifically allowing only 80, 443, and your custom SSH port.

items.title: CrowdSec Security Engine
items.description: Install CrowdSec to detect and block malicious IPs based on behavior, sharing threat intelligence with other users globally.

items.title: Fail2Ban for SSH Protection
items.description: Configure Fail2Ban to monitor auth logs and temporarily ban IP addresses that show signs of brute-force login attempts.

items.title: Nginx Proxy Manager
items.description: A Docker-based GUI for managing Nginx reverse proxy settings, ideal for developers who prefer a visual interface for SSL and routing.

items.title: Traefik for Docker Orchestration
items.description: Use Traefik to automatically discover Docker containers and route traffic based on labels, perfect for dynamic VPS environments.

items.title: WireGuard for Site-to-Site VPN
items.description: Manually configure WireGuard tunnels to connect VPS instances across different providers (e.g., Hetzner to AWS) securely.

items.title: SSH Key-Only Authentication
items.description: Disable password authentication in /etc/ssh/sshd_config to eliminate the risk of password-based credential stuffing.

items.title: Cloudflare Tunnel (cloudflared)
items.description: Expose web services on your VPS to the internet without opening any inbound firewall ports by using an outbound tunnel to Cloudflare.

items.title: Kamal (formerly MRSK)
items.description: Deploy Dockerized applications to any VPS using SSH. Kamal handles zero-downtime rollouts and load balancer configuration without a heavy PaaS.

items.title: Coolify Self-Hosted PaaS
items.description: An open-source alternative to Heroku/Vercel that runs on your VPS. It manages deployments, databases, and backups via a web UI.

items.title: Dokku (The Smallest PaaS)
items.description: A Docker-powered PaaS that lets you push code via Git (git push dokku master) to build and deploy applications automatically.

items.title: GitHub Actions Self-Hosted Runners
items.description: Install a GitHub runner on your VPS to execute CI/CD jobs locally, avoiding the cost of GitHub-hosted runner minutes.

items.title: Netdata Real-time Monitoring
items.description: Install Netdata for per-second monitoring of CPU, RAM, and disk I/O with zero configuration and a built-in dashboard.

items.title: Prometheus and Grafana Stack
items.description: Set up a centralized monitoring server to scrape metrics from multiple VPS nodes and visualize them in custom Grafana dashboards.

items.title: Watchtower for Docker Updates
items.description: A container that monitors your running Docker containers and automatically restarts them if a new image is pushed to the registry.

items.title: Uptime Kuma for Status Pages
items.description: Self-host a monitoring tool to track the uptime of your services and receive notifications via Telegram, Discord, or Slack.

items.title: BorgBackup for Data Integrity
items.description: Use Borg for deduplicating, compressed, and encrypted backups of your application data to an offsite Storage Box.

items.title: Logrotate Configuration
items.description: Ensure /etc/logrotate.conf is configured for all application logs to prevent your VPS disk from filling up with old text files.

pro_tips: Always use a non-root user with sudo privileges for daily operations to prevent accidental system-wide damage.
Enable 'Backups' at the provider level (Hetzner/OVH) for a full-image recovery option, in addition to your application-level database backups.
When selecting a VPS, prioritize NVMe SSD storage over standard SSDs for significantly better database performance and build speeds.
Set up a 'Swap' file (usually 2GB-4GB) on smaller VPS instances to prevent applications from crashing during memory-intensive deployments.
Use a dedicated 'Management VPS' or 'Jump Box' to access your production nodes, keeping the production servers' SSH ports closed to the general internet.
